textDisplay:
Sly (and Arnold) understand the psyche of The Action Crowd better than anyone.
Ever.
That's why it still works -
Curious re: the Score, i.e. who wrote it, etc. - the Scores are always exceptional (Sly sees to that).
The other advantage to Sly later in his career?
(Rocky sequels as well)
There's always added wisdom - unusual insight(s) and remorse - i.e. Sly looking-back.
I was gonna go down to Highland Park (Eagle Rock area)  - watch it over there - it's an almost empty theater
(at noon) - and it's like discount prices FYI (people in L.A.).    You can think, focus,
learn from Sly re: camera placement, sound, business, staging, PERFORMANCE, scene construction etc.
 - Sly's like his own film school
(where I've been back in NYC)
.
.

textDisplay:
The Real Rambo lived in a Ranch named Rancho San Jose on  November 13 2010 a Mexican State of Tamaulipas 15 kilometers Near the Capital of La Victoria
The Zeta Cartel had gone a day before to this ranch and told the owner to handover the ranch and he had 24 hours to abandon the ranch or face the consequences. 
Don Alejo Garza send all of his workers home and he transformed his ranch into a fort.he was a hunter and had hunting rifles and some other hand guns and he position his arsenal in strategic points as well other traps that he had set up for the Sicarios.
Don Alejo had witnessed how his neighbors where also victims of the Sicarios.
Unlike Don Alejo the neighbors just abandoned the ranches.
Don Alejo confronted 30 Sicarios and killed 4 and wounded 2 more Sicarios   they launch granades at the ranch and thats how Don Alejo died .
He was 77 years old .

textDisplay:
Apparently it was lost on you that his "niece" (or whatever she is) happens to look Latino?  And we have "big bad" cartel bosses to root against who are exploiting and terrorizing  ordinary law abiding America loving Latinos?  Yeah, some of that is acceptable.  But it's still the woke "victim hood" mentality creeping into a character's motivations who really is fighting a war with his country because his country doesn't respect his abilities and considers him expendable. 



* First blood -- get out of my alll-American town you vagrant drifter ... your Congressional Medal of Honor is no good here!; 

* Rambo FB2 -- go be our patsy in the rice patties so we can say there are no POW Americans... oh, you found some... enjoy staying behind
* Rambo 3 -- enjoying millitary prison Rambo... that's your reward for saving those POWs, and by the way, that colonel father-figure you trained with... yeah, we threw him under the bus to the Soviets in Afghanistan. 

*Rambo (2008) -- Rambo you can't come home, so hang out in Thailand, like Nick in the Deer Hunter, and we're just going to showcase how your country doesn't care about the war crimes and humanitarian crimes going on around you.   But this time we'll give you a nice white female missionary to save, and saving her is going to somehow lead you to resolve your differences with your country?! 

* Last Blood -- Ok, Rambo... kill the cartel members and save the foxy looking Latina.  The guys making the video games have great success with this plot formula so it should work for you too! 



This is how an action character is slowly sucked dry of his soul.

textDisplay: @gdeangelkick, nice, but I don't think you hit the nail. See Apocalypse Now came out '79 criticizing the shit show in Vietnam. First Blood in '82 shot light on conflict between those who came back and the sanitized All-American bubble. Then came Reagan and the DoD was all in to forget Apocalypse Now: out came Top Gun in '86. The best pro-military-commercial of the '80. FB2 in '85 was pandering to the idea that "Government is the Problem" - btw. check out Ghostbusters '84 on that trope .. "Rambo" was just the perfect IP at hand. R3 was used again in '88 to emphasis: we are the good guys, the communists are the Evil Empire - look at those poor Afghans fighting for freedom. .. I thought R4 was just poor try to make money out of an old IP. Just an other remake.  .. Hey .. then comes Trump .. and the Trumpists .. that's gold .. Rambo fights Mexicans at the home front .. that's gonna make them boku bucks. Remember: Hollywood likes to play it safe nowadays.

textDisplay: The original story sees the death of Rambo at the hands of Trautman, an ending I have always felt fit the character so much better and the narrative the movie was trying to push. That being help our veterans before it is too late, as evidenced by John himself in his descent into madness. The rest after that have been typical action movies, not that I dislike them. However the original was so much more than an action movie.
textOriginal: The original story sees the death of Rambo at the hands of Trautman, an ending I have always felt fit the character so much better and the narrative the movie was trying to push. That being help our veterans before it is too late, as evidenced by John himself in his descent into madness. The rest after that have been typical action movies, not that I dislike them. However the original was so much more than an action movie.

textDisplay:
First Blood was great, Rambo 2/3 were dumb but fun, Rambo 4 felt like a proper sequel to First Blood because I felt like it was paying more attention to the trauma that Rambo's been dealing with since Vietnam. I really liked the ending of Rambo 4 because it ended in a way that made the whole thing feel like a complete arc and he's at a point where he can start to properly heal.


It'll be interesting to see if they go with a more First Blood or Rambo 2/3 way with Rambo's character. Hopefully it'll be more like First Blood, but I don't mind dumb fun (like with the Expendables - brilliant movies).
textOriginal:
First Blood was great, Rambo 2/3 were dumb but fun, Rambo 4 felt like a proper sequel to First Blood because I felt like it was paying more attention to the trauma that Rambo's been dealing with since Vietnam. I really liked the ending of Rambo 4 because it ended in a way that made the whole thing feel like a complete arc and he's at a point where he can start to properly heal.


It'll be interesting to see if they go with a more First Blood or Rambo 2/3 way with Rambo's character. Hopefully it'll be more like First Blood, but I don't mind dumb fun (like with the Expendables - brilliant movies).

First blood, commando, stop or my mom will shoot,  true lies, etc etc..
By the time i was born, the 70s and 80s classics were all released and beloved, while some of the weirder 90s and early millennium stuff were coming..
Last action hero, judge dredd, jingle all the way, and ANTZ..
Im SO excited to see Rambo return.
Yes he's old. So what it's a very old franchise?
Rambo kicks ass

The franchise that needs to die? Terminator 

I thought salvation was decent

textDisplay:
"A man's man"? An ex-soldier with severe PTSD trying to live a quiet peaceful life building Buddhist temples, helping the poor and downtrodden and oppressed, and giving other people the peace and hope they deserve all while showing ignorant Americans how pathetically insular their middle class lives are? I mean, yeah that's a great idea of masculinity, actually.

Seriously, dude, John Rambo is a history lesson in everything that is wrong with your idea of a "man's man".

I mean, yeah that's a great idea of masculinity, actually.

Seriously, dude, John Rambo is a history lesson in everything that is wrong with your idea of a "man's man".

textDisplay:
Col. Trautman: I don't think you understand. I didn't come to rescue Rambo from you. I came here to rescue you from him.
Sheriff Teasle: [clearly taken aback by Trautman's words] Well, we all appreciate your concern Colonel, I will try to be extra careful!
Col. Trautman: You picked the wrong man to push.
Sheriff Teasle: [walking back into the tent] No, Trautman, he picked the wrong man!
Col. Trautman: [following him in] That boy's a heart attack! He may be the best the Special Forces ever produced. Now whatever you're planning to throw at him here, he's been through a whole lot worse, in a lot worse places that this. I'm just amazed he allowed any of your posse to live.
Teasle: Is that right?
Trautman: Strictly speaking, he slipped up. You're lucky to be breathing.
Teasle: Colonel, you came out here to find out why one of your machines blew a gasket!
Trautman: You don't seem to want to accept the fact that you're dealing with an expert in guerrilla warfare, with a man who's the best! With guns, with knives, with his bare hands! A man who's been trained to ignore pain! To ignore weather! To live off the land! To eat things that would make a billy goat puke! In Vietnam his job was to dispose of enemy personnel... to kill, period! Win by attrition... well Rambo was THE BEST!

Col. Trautman: You picked the wrong man to push.
Sheriff Teasle: [walking back into the tent] No, Trautman, he picked the wrong man!
Col. Trautman: [following him in] That boy's a heart attack! He may be the best the Special Forces ever produced. Now whatever you're planning to throw at him here, he's been through a whole lot worse, in a lot worse places that this. I'm just amazed he allowed any of your posse to live.
Teasle: Is that right?
Trautman: Strictly speaking, he slipped up. You're lucky to be breathing.
Teasle: Colonel, you came out here to find out why one of your machines blew a gasket!
Trautman: You don't seem to want to accept the fact that you're dealing with an expert in guerrilla warfare, with a man who's the best! With guns, with knives, with his bare hands! A man who's been trained to ignore pain! To ignore weather! To live off the land! To eat things that would make a billy goat puke! textDisplay: Hmm. Definitely a nicely edited trailer in any case, and it'll be good to see some mindless action with a bit of budgetary oomph behind it. That said, this film seems completely out of sorts compared to the first four. Rambo's always been about this history-less, stoic soldier who does not get attached to anyone except his CO, Trautman. From the very first film almost no reference has ever been made to Rambo's pre-Vietnam history. This has been a defining feature of the character - the idea that one could believe that he came out of the womb with that killer instinct, that you could never imagine him as a child, with family, happy. That's what made the final shot of the postbox in Rambo 4 so jarring - it was meant to represent Rambo ending his war and finally finding peace.
textOriginal: Hmm. Definitely a nicely edited trailer in any case, and it'll be good to see some mindless action with a bit of budgetary oomph behind it. That said, this film seems completely out of sorts compared to the first four. Rambo's always been about this history-less, stoic soldier who does not get attached to anyone except his CO, Trautman. From the very first film almost no reference has ever been made to Rambo's pre-Vietnam history. This has been a defining feature of the character - the idea that one could believe that he came out of the womb with that killer instinct, that you could never imagine him as a child, with family, happy. That's what made the final shot of the postbox in Rambo 4 so jarring - it was meant to represent Rambo ending his war and finally finding peace.

textDisplay:
"When you're pushed killing's as easy as breathing." 
 "live for nothing or die for something.... your call."
 "Nothing is over! nothing! you just don't turn it off!."  
"What do you say John?"......."Fuck Em" 
"Murdock .... i'm coming to get you" 
"I could have killed 'em all, I could kill you. In town you're the law, out here it's me. Don't push it. Don't push it or I'll give you a war you won't believe."
 "I want them to know that death is coming, and that there is nothing they can do to stop it"
"I'm gonna tear you apart......this is what it feels like"
"I finally came home to defend the only family I've ever known"
 "All shes got is me"
"You started this i'll end it"
"I want Revenge" 
 Heroes never die.... They just reload.   God would have mercy. John Rambo won't!.    One war against one man. What most people call hell, he calls home.
They sent him on a mission and set him up to fail, but they made one mistake. They forgot who they were dealing with: Rambo.
No man, no law, no war can stop him. 
Looking forward to seeing Rambo kill the bad guys one more time. Rated Hard R.

textDisplay: Disappointing trailer the footage they show isn't that much exciting as in the previous trailer with a dark version of the old town road song, most of it is already shown in the previous trailer mixed in with some little bit of new footage that didn't need to be shown at all like when the Spanish girl gets drugged and kidnapped as to why he comes out of retirement or when the Cartel pulling up to his house as he busy preparing his boobytraps in his barn and house and tunnel catacombs and a scene reminiscent of Rambo 3 scene where he talks on the walkie talkie threatening the cartel leader that he's dealing with to scare him, a scene where he carries the spanish girl out of the apartment complex that she's being held hostage in and footage from the previous films like First Blood part 2, Rambo 3 and 4 with an old projector film tape filter effect with Trautman lines dubbed in there talking about what  Rambo is capable of and how much of a expert war warrior that he is with flashback glimpse of the scene when he was younger getting ready to stab Murdoch in the face with his iconic knife as he pin him down to his office desk in First Blood Part 2 to his older self doing the same, the way how this trailer was put together felt more like a TV spot than a real second trailer, it should have been renamed Rambo: Last Blood TV Spot #1 instead and this trailer honestly compare to the teaser already giveaway 97% of the whole plot three act structure of the film and i terms of action set pieces I believe it's only going to be that sequence where he kills them left and right at his house home alone style with weapons and boobytraps, the only thing they haven't show is the ending on whether he lives or dies (Like Logan) which Lionsgate will possibly show it in a TV spot without any of us noticing or maybe just maybe the ending could be that scene where he stabs that guy he have hanged up in his barn and if so what's the point of going to the theater to see it now ? My suggestion wait til someone put it out bootleg to watch it.
